name: App boots as a working React SPA
description: The reconstructed app runs locally and renders a complete single-page experience without fatal build or runtime errors.
verification: Start the app and confirm the main page renders fully; run a production build successfully.

name: Recovered summary view is present
description: A top-level summary or hero section communicates the page purpose and recovered framing from the original draft.
verification: Inspect the top of the page and confirm summary content is visible on initial load.

name: Phase navigation exists
description: The app exposes clear navigation or jump controls for the major phases implied by the original component.
verification: Use the page navigation controls and confirm each major phase can be reached.

name: Each major phase has a detailed section
description: Every major phase recovered from the damaged draft is represented as its own readable detailed section.
verification: Compare the implemented sections against the recovered plan/spec and confirm all major phases appear.

name: Recovered headings and copy are preserved
description: The builder preserves the intended labels, headings, and explanatory content from the corrupted source wherever recoverable.
verification: Spot-check rendered copy against the recovered source mapping and confirm preservation of core content.

name: Dashboard summary blocks render coherently
description: Dashboard-like cards, metrics, or visual summary regions implied by the original draft are reconstructed into readable UI blocks.
verification: Inspect dashboard sections and confirm they render structured summary information rather than placeholders or broken markup.

name: Interactive calculator section is implemented where implied
description: At least one calculator-style interaction from the damaged draft is restored as a working input/output experience where the original intent is clear.
verification: Enter values into calculator controls and confirm outputs update without errors.

name: Calculator state updates are stable
description: Calculator interactions handle multiple input changes predictably and do not create NaN, blank, or crashing states during normal use.
verification: Change inputs repeatedly and confirm outputs remain valid and the page remains stable.

name: Single-page information flow is coherent
description: The page reads as one intentional product experience from summary to deeper details, not as disconnected recovered fragments.
verification: Perform a top-to-bottom walkthrough and confirm the sections follow a coherent order and narrative.

name: Responsive layout works at common widths
description: The reconstructed page remains usable at desktop and mobile widths with readable text, reachable controls, and no catastrophic overlap.
verification: Check at one desktop width and one mobile width and confirm readability and interaction viability.

name: Visual design is polished and non-placeholder
description: The final page has a credible, presentation-ready visual treatment appropriate for a finance/futures concept and avoids obvious scaffold styling.
verification: Inspect the live UI and confirm consistent spacing, hierarchy, and deliberate visual styling.

name: Production build succeeds
description: The app can be built for production without unresolved compile-time issues.
verification: Run the production build command and confirm it completes successfully.

name: Deployment target is GitHub-hosted live link
description: The finished app is deployed through Ryan’s GitHub presence and exposed at a reachable live URL.
verification: Open the deployed URL and confirm the app loads publicly.

name: Recovered assumptions are contained
description: Where the damaged draft forced interpretation, the resulting UI stays close to the apparent original intent and does not introduce unrelated product behavior.
verification: Review any assumption-driven sections and confirm they align with the spec’s documented reconstruction strategy.
